session_hint: the GitHub Actions pipeline slowdown investigation
question: What did we rule out when investigating why our GitHub Actions pipeline started taking 42 minutes?
turns: Our GitHub Actions CI pipeline ballooned from 18 to 42 minutes after last week's merge. First suspect: the test suite grew substantially — we added 200 new tests that week.
Timed the test step in isolation: it takes 9 minutes, same as before the regression. The new tests are fast unit tests adding under 30 seconds total. Test suite size is not the cause, ruling it out.

Thought the npm install step might be skipping cache and fetching from the registry. Checked the cache hit logs: the actions/cache step restores at a 97% hit rate every run. Dependency install is consistently 45 seconds, not the bottleneck either.

Traced it to the Docker build step: a Dockerfile refactor moved the COPY . . instruction before the RUN npm ci layer. Now every code change busts the dependency install layer inside Docker, causing a full npm ci on every push. Reordered the Dockerfile so COPY only copies package.json first, then RUN npm ci, then COPY . . — pipeline dropped back to 20 minutes.
